title: Braintree API keys should not be disclosed
description: Braintree (a PayPal company) is a payment processing platform used to accept credit cards and digital payments. Braintree credentials include Merchant ID, Public Key, and Private Key. Exposing these credentials allows unauthorized financial transactions, access to customer payment data, and potential PCI-DSS compliance violations.
recommendation: Remove Braintree credentials from source code. Store Merchant ID, Public Key, and Private Key in environment variables or a secrets manager. Never hardcode payment gateway credentials. Rotate exposed keys immediately in the Braintree Control Panel.
impact: Unauthorized payment processing, access to customer credit card data, potential financial fraud, and PCI-DSS compliance violations.

checks.message: Braintree Private Key detected. Braintree private keys authenticate server-side API calls for payment processing. Exposing this key allows unauthorized charges, refunds, subscription management, and access to customer payment method vaults containing credit card data. This represents a critical PCI-DSS compliance violation. Immediately rotate in the Braintree Control Panel and report per PCI-DSS incident response requirements.

Braintree Public Key detected. While Braintree public keys are less sensitive than private keys, they are used together with the private key for API authentication. Exposing the public key combined with a private key (commonly found nearby in configuration) constitutes complete API credentials for unauthorized payment processing. Rotate all Braintree credentials immediately.

Braintree gateway initialization with hardcoded private key detected. Hardcoded Braintree credentials in gateway initialization allow full payment processing access including unauthorized charges, refunds, and access to stored payment methods. This is a PCI-DSS violation requiring immediate credential rotation and security incident reporting.
